1	This module contains a simple AXI Direct Memory Access (DMA) component for streaming
2	data from FPGA to DDR memory.
3	Sometimes called "AXI DMA S2MM".
4	The implementation is optimized for very low resource usage and maximum AXI throughput.
5	
6	See the website for an overview and documentation of what is available:
7	https://hdl-modules.com/modules/dma_axi_write_simple/dma_axi_write_simple.html
8

file.content:
This module contains a simple AXI Direct Memory Access (DMA) component for streaming
data from FPGA to DDR memory.
Sometimes called "AXI DMA S2MM".
The implementation is optimized for very low resource usage and maximum AXI throughput.

See the website for an overview and documentation of what is available:
https://hdl-modules.com/modules/dma_axi_write_simple/dma_axi_write_simple.html

content.content:
1	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2	-- Copyright (c) Lukas Vik. All rights reserved.
3	--
4	-- This file is part of the hdl-modules project, a collection of reusable, high-quality,
5	-- peer-reviewed VHDL building blocks.
6	-- https://hdl-modules.com
7	-- https://github.com/hdl-modules/hdl-modules
8	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9	-- Main implementation of the simple DMA functionality.
10	-- This entity is not suitable for instantiation in a user design, use instead e.g.
11	-- :ref:`dma_axi_write_simple.dma_axi_write_simple_axi_lite`.
12	--
13	--
14	-- Packet length
15	-- _____________
16	--
17	-- The ``packet_length_beats`` generic specifies the packet length in terms of number of
18	-- input ``stream`` beats.
19	-- When one packet of streaming data has been written to DDR,
20	-- the ``write_done`` interrupt will trigger and the ``buffer_written_address`` register
21	-- will be updated.
22	-- This indicates to the software that there is data in the buffer that can be read.
23	--
24	-- .. note::
25	--   The packet length is a compile-time parameter.
26	--   It can not be changed during runtime and there is no support for writing or clearing
27	--   partial packets.
28	--
29	--   This saves a lot of resources and is part of the simple nature of this DMA core.
30	--
31	-- If the packet length specified by the user equates more than one maximum-length AXI burst,
32	-- the core will perform burst splitting internally.
33	--
34	--
35	-- Data width conversion
36	-- _____________________
37	--
38	-- The core supports data width conversion between the input ``stream`` and the AXI bus.
39	-- If the ``stream_data_width`` and ``axi_data_width`` are not equal,
40	-- :ref:`common.width_conversion` will be instantiated for a lightweight conversion.
41	-- Note that ``axi_data_width`` must be the native width of the AXI port.
42	--
43	--
44	-- .. _dma_axi_write_simple_resource_usage:
45	--
46	-- Resource usage
47	-- ______________
48	--
49	-- The core has a simple design with the goal of low resource utilization in mind.
50	-- See :ref:`dma_axi_write_simple.dma_axi_write_simple_axi_lite.resource_utilization`
51	-- for some build numbers.
52	-- These numbers are incredibly low compared to some other implementations.
53	--
54	-- The special case when ``packet_length_beats`` is 1 has an optimized implementation that gives
55	-- even lower resource usage than the general case.
56	-- This comes at the cost of quite poor memory performance, since every data beat becomes and
57	-- AXI burst in that case.
58	--
59	--
60	-- .. _dma_axi_write_simple_throughput:
61	--
62	-- AXI/data throughput
63	-- ___________________
64	--
65	-- The core has a one-cycle overhead per packet.
66	-- Meaning that for each packet, the input ``stream`` will stall (``stream_ready = 0``) for one
67	-- clock cycle.
68	-- This is assuming that ``AWREADY`` and ``WREADY`` are high.
69	-- If they are not, their stall will be propagated to the ``stream``.
70	--
71	-- This performance should be enough for even the most demanding applications.
72	-- The one-cycle overhead could theoretically be optimized away, but it is quite likely
73	-- that downstream AXI interconnect infrastructure has some overhead for each address
74	-- transaction anyway.
75	-- I.e. the one-cycle overhead in this core is probably not limiting the throughput overall.
76	--
77	-- If the memory buffer is full, the ``stream`` will stall until there is space.
78	-- When the software writes an updated ``buffer_read_address`` register indicating available space,
79	-- the ``stream`` will start after two clock cycles.
80	--
81	--
82	-- AXI behavior
83	-- ____________
84	--
85	-- The core is designed to be as well-behaved as possible in an AXI sense:
86	--
87	-- 1. AXI bursts of the maximum length possible will be used.
88	--
89	-- 2. The ``AW`` transaction is only initiated once we have at least one ``W`` beat available.
90	--
91	-- 3. ``BREADY`` is always high.
92	--
93	-- This gives very good AXI performance.
94	--
95	-- W channel block
96	-- ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
97	--
98	-- Related to bullet point 2 above, the core does NOT accumulate a whole burst in order to
99	-- guarantee no holes in the data.
100	-- Meaning, it is possible that an ``AW`` and a few ``W`` transactions  happen, but then the
101	-- ``stream`` might stop for a while and block the AXI bus before the burst is finished.
102	--
103	-- This can be problematic if the downstream AXI slave is a crossbar/interconnect that
104	-- arbitrates between multiple AXI masters.
105	--
106	-- It is up to the user to make sure that either,
107	--
108	-- 1. The ``stream`` stopping within a packet is rare enough that sufficient AXI performance
109	--    is reached.
110	-- 2. Or, the downstream AXI slave can handle holes without impacting performance.
111	--    :ref:`axi.axi_write_throttle` is designed to help with this.
112	--
113	-- AXI3
114	-- ~~~~
115	--
116	-- Setting the ``enable_axi3`` generic will make the core compliant with AXI3 instead of AXI4.
117	-- The core does not use any of the ID fields (``AWID``, ``WID``, ``BID``) so the only difference
118	-- is the burst length limitation.
119	--
120	--
121	-- ``write_done`` interrupt aggregation
122	-- ____________________________________
123	--
124	-- The ``write_done`` interrupt bit is triggered every time a packet has been written to memory
125	-- (see :ref:`dma_axi_write_simple.register_interface`).
126	-- If an interrupt-based control flow is used, this can lead to a high interrupt rate if
127	-- packets are small but data rate is high.
128	-- This can be problematic for the CPU.
129	--
130	-- The generics ``write_done_aggregate_count`` and ``write_done_aggregate_ticks`` can be used to
131	-- aggregate the interrupt event so it is triggered more sparsely.
132	-- See :ref:`common.event_aggregator` for details.
133	-- Commonly, both generics are set to ensure that the interrupt is triggered:
134	--
135	-- 1. Not too often (too little data per interrupt, too high overhead in interrupt manager).
136	-- 2. Not too seldom (too much data per interrupt, buffer might fill up and stream might stall).
137	-- 3. Not too delayed (too high latency in data flow).
138	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
139	
140	library ieee;
141	use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
142	use ieee.numeric_std.all;
143	
144	library axi;
145	use axi.axi_pkg.all;
146	
147	library axi_lite;
148	use axi_lite.axi_lite_pkg.all;
149	
150	library common;
151	use common.types_pkg.all;
152	
153	library math;
154	use math.math_pkg.is_power_of_two;
155	
156	library register_file;
157	use register_file.register_file_pkg.all;
158	
159	library ring_buffer;
160	use ring_buffer.ring_buffer_write_simple_pkg.all;
161	
162	use work.dma_axi_write_simple_register_record_pkg.all;
163	use work.dma_axi_write_simple_regs_pkg.all;
164	
165	
166	entity dma_axi_write_simple is
167	  generic (
168	    -- The width of the AXI AWADDR field as well as all the ring buffer addresses
169	    -- handled internally.
170	    address_width : axi_address_width_t;
171	    -- The data width of the 'stream' interface.
172	    stream_data_width : axi_data_width_t;
173	    -- The width of the AXI WDATA field.
174	    -- Must be the native width of the AXI port, we do not support narrow bursts.
175	    axi_data_width : axi_data_width_t;
176	    -- The number of beats on the 'stream' interface that are accumulated before
177	    -- being written to memory.
178	    -- Increasing this up to the maximum burst size will improve memory performance.
179	    -- It will also decrease the frequency of 'write_done' interrupts.
180	    -- But note that there is no support for writing partial packets.
181	    -- If the 'stream' stops in the middle of a packet, there is not way to write or clear the
182	    -- accumulated data.
183	    packet_length_beats : positive;
184	    -- Enable AXI3 instead of AXI4, with the limitations that this implies.
185	    enable_axi3 : boolean;
186	    -- If set, the 'write_done' interrupt bit will be delayed until this many packets have
187	    -- been written to memory.
188	    -- See header/documentation for details.
189	    write_done_aggregate_count : positive;
190	    -- If set, the 'write_done' interrupt bit will be delayed and sent out at a periodic interval
191	    -- given by this clock tick count.
192	    -- See header/documentation for details.
193	    write_done_aggregate_ticks : positive
194	  );
195	  port (
196	    clk : in std_ulogic;
197	    --# {{}}
198	    stream_ready : out std_ulogic := '0';
199	    stream_valid : in std_ulogic;
200	    stream_data : in std_ulogic_vector(stream_data_width - 1 downto 0);
201	    --# {{}}
202	    regs_up : out dma_axi_write_simple_regs_up_t := dma_axi_write_simple_regs_up_init;
203	    regs_down : in dma_axi_write_simple_regs_down_t;
204	    interrupt : out std_ulogic := '0';
205	    --# {{}}
206	    axi_write_m2s : out axi_write_m2s_t := axi_write_m2s_init;
207	    axi_write_s2m : in axi_write_s2m_t
208	  );
209	end entity;
210	
211	architecture a of dma_axi_write_simple is
212	
213	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
214	  -- Generic constants.
215	  constant stream_data_width_bytes : positive := stream_data_width / 8;
216	  constant packet_length_bytes : positive := packet_length_beats * stream_data_width_bytes;
217	
218	  constant axi_data_width_bytes : positive := axi_data_width / 8;
219	  constant packet_length_axi_beats : positive := packet_length_bytes / axi_data_width_bytes;
220	
221	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
222	  -- Signals.
223	  signal interrupt_sources : register_t := (others => '0');
224	
225	  signal ring_buffer_status : ring_buffer_write_simple_status_t := (
226	    ring_buffer_write_simple_status_idle_no_error
227	  );
228	
229	  signal axi_ready, axi_valid : std_ulogic := '0';
230	  signal axi_data : std_ulogic_vector(axi_data_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
231	
232	begin
233	
234	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
235	  assert sanity_check_axi_data_width(data_width=>axi_data_width)
236	    report "Invalid AXI data width. See above."
237	    severity failure;
238	
239	  -- We check the data width using the same function, for simplicity.
240	  assert sanity_check_axi_data_width(data_width=>stream_data_width)
241	    report "Invalid stream data width. See above."
242	    severity failure;
243	
244	  assert stream_data_width mod axi_data_width = 0 or axi_data_width mod stream_data_width = 0
245	    report "Data width ratio must be a whole number of beats."
246	    severity failure;
247	
248	  assert is_power_of_two(packet_length_beats)
249	    report "Packet length must be a power of two for efficient calculations."
250	    severity failure;
251	
252	  assert packet_length_bytes mod axi_data_width_bytes = 0
253	    report "Packet length must be a whole number of AXI beats."
254	    severity failure;
255	
256	  assert is_power_of_two(packet_length_bytes / axi_data_width_bytes)
257	    report "Packet length must be a power-of-two number of AXI beats."
258	    severity failure;
259	
260	
261	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
262	  interrupt_register_block : block
263	    signal write_done : std_ulogic := '0';
264	    signal clear, status : register_t := (others => '0');
265	  begin
266	
267	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
268	    interrupt_register_inst : entity register_file.interrupt_register
269	      port map (
270	        clk => clk,
271	        --
272	        sources => interrupt_sources,
273	        mask => regs_down.interrupt_mask,
274	        clear => clear,
275	        --
276	        status => status,
277	        trigger => interrupt
278	      );
279	
280	    write_done <= axi_write_m2s.b.ready and axi_write_s2m.b.valid;
281	
282	    interrupt_sources(dma_axi_write_simple_interrupt_status_write_error) <= (
283	      axi_write_m2s.b.ready
284	      and axi_write_s2m.b.valid
285	      and to_sl(axi_write_s2m.b.resp /= axi_resp_okay)
286	    );
287	
288	    interrupt_sources(dma_axi_write_simple_interrupt_status_start_address_unaligned_error) <= (
289	      ring_buffer_status.start_address_unaligned
290	    );
291	
292	    interrupt_sources(dma_axi_write_simple_interrupt_status_end_address_unaligned_error) <= (
293	      ring_buffer_status.end_address_unaligned
294	    );
295	
296	    interrupt_sources(dma_axi_write_simple_interrupt_status_read_address_unaligned_error) <= (
297	      ring_buffer_status.read_address_unaligned
298	    );
299	
300	    clear <= to_slv(regs_down.interrupt_status);
301	
302	    regs_up.interrupt_status <= to_dma_axi_write_simple_interrupt_status(status);
303	
304	
305	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
306	    -- Optionally aggregate, is a passthrough if generics are at default value.
307	    event_aggregator_inst : entity common.event_aggregator
308	      generic map (
309	        event_count => write_done_aggregate_count,
310	        tick_count => write_done_aggregate_ticks
311	      )
312	      port map (
313	        clk => clk,
314	        --
315	        input_event => write_done,
316	        aggregated_event => interrupt_sources(dma_axi_write_simple_interrupt_status_write_done)
317	      );
318	
319	  end block;
320	
321	
322	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
323	  width_conversion_gen : if stream_data_width /= axi_data_width generate
324	
325	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
326	    width_conversion_inst : entity common.width_conversion
327	      generic map (
328	        input_width => stream_data'length,
329	        output_width => axi_data'length
330	      )
331	      port map (
332	        clk => clk,
333	        --
334	        input_ready => stream_ready,
335	        input_valid => stream_valid,
336	        input_data => stream_data,
337	        --
338	        output_ready => axi_ready,
339	        output_valid => axi_valid,
340	        output_data => axi_data
341	      );
342	
343	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
344	  else generate
345	
346	    stream_ready <= axi_ready;
347	    axi_valid <= stream_valid;
348	    axi_data <= stream_data;
349	
350	  end generate;
351	
352	
353	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
354	  axi_block : block
355	    function get_num_axi_bursts_per_packet return positive is
356	      constant max_axi_burst_length_beats : positive := get_max_burst_length_beats(
357	        enable_axi3=>enable_axi3
358	      );
359	      constant max_axi_burst_length_bytes : positive := (
360	        max_axi_burst_length_beats * axi_data_width_bytes
361	      );
362	
363	      constant packet_fits_in_one_axi_burst : boolean := (
364	        packet_length_axi_beats <= max_axi_burst_length_beats
365	      );
366	    begin
367	      if packet_fits_in_one_axi_burst then
368	        return 1;
369	      end if;
370	
371	      assert packet_length_bytes mod max_axi_burst_length_bytes = 0
372	        report (
373	            "When burst splitting, packet length must be a "
374	            & "whole number of max-length AXI bursts."
375	          )
376	          severity failure;
377	
378	      return packet_length_bytes / max_axi_burst_length_bytes;
379	    end function;
380	    constant num_axi_bursts_per_packet : positive := get_num_axi_bursts_per_packet;
381	
382	    constant axi_burst_length_beats : positive := (
383	      packet_length_axi_beats / num_axi_bursts_per_packet
384	    );
385	    constant axi_burst_length_bytes : positive := axi_burst_length_beats * axi_data_width_bytes;
386	
387	    signal segment_ready, segment_valid : std_ulogic := '0';
388	    signal segment_address : u_unsigned(address_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
389	  begin
390	
391	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
392	    print_things : process
393	    begin
394	      report "packet_length_beats = " & integer'image(packet_length_beats);
395	      report "packet_length_axi_beats = " & integer'image(packet_length_axi_beats);
396	      report "num_axi_bursts_per_packet = " & integer'image(num_axi_bursts_per_packet);
397	      report "axi_burst_length_beats = " & integer'image(axi_burst_length_beats);
398	
399	      wait;
400	    end process;
401	
402	
403	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
404	    ring_buffer_block : block
405	      signal buffer_start_address, buffer_end_address, buffer_written_address, buffer_read_address :
406	        u_unsigned(address_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
407	
408	      -- If we are doing burst splitting, not every 'BVALID' marks the end of a packet.
409	      signal is_last_burst_in_packet : std_ulogic := '0';
410	      signal write_done : std_ulogic := '0';
411	    begin
412	
413	      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
414	      ring_buffer_write_simple_inst : entity ring_buffer.ring_buffer_write_simple
415	        generic map (
416	          address_width => address_width,
417	          -- We pop one address 'segment' per AXI burst, which might be multiple per packet.
418	          segment_length_bytes => axi_burst_length_bytes,
419	          -- Update 'buffer_written_address' once the whole packet (not the 'segment')
420	          -- has been written.
421	          segments_per_packet => num_axi_bursts_per_packet
422	        )
423	        port map (
424	          clk => clk,
425	          --
426	          enable => regs_down.config.enable,
427	          --
428	          buffer_start_address => buffer_start_address,
429	          buffer_end_address => buffer_end_address,
430	          buffer_written_address => buffer_written_address,
431	          buffer_read_address => buffer_read_address,
432	          --
433	          segment_ready => segment_ready,
434	          segment_valid => segment_valid,
435	          segment_address => segment_address,
436	          --
437	          write_done => write_done,
438	          --
439	          status => ring_buffer_status
440	        );
441	
442	      buffer_start_address <= u_unsigned(
443	        regs_down.buffer_start_address(buffer_start_address'range)
444	      );
445	      buffer_end_address <= u_unsigned(regs_down.buffer_end_address(buffer_end_address'range));
446	      buffer_read_address <= u_unsigned(regs_down.buffer_read_address(buffer_read_address'range));
447	
448	      regs_up.buffer_written_address(buffer_written_address'range) <= std_ulogic_vector(
449	        buffer_written_address
450	      );
451	
452	
453	      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
454	      assign_last_inst : entity common.assign_last
455	        generic map (
456	          packet_length_beats => num_axi_bursts_per_packet
457	        )
458	        port map (
459	          clk => clk,
460	          --
461	          ready => axi_write_m2s.b.ready,
462	          valid => axi_write_s2m.b.valid,
463	          last => is_last_burst_in_packet
464	        );
465	
466	      write_done <= (
467	        axi_write_m2s.b.ready and axi_write_s2m.b.valid and is_last_burst_in_packet
468	      );
469	
470	    end block;
471	
472	    -- Note that no AWID is set. Hence no WID has to be set in AXI3 mode either.
473	
474	    axi_write_m2s.aw.len <= to_len(burst_length_beats=>axi_burst_length_beats);
475	    axi_write_m2s.aw.size <= to_size(data_width_bits=>axi_data_width);
476	    axi_write_m2s.aw.burst <= axi_a_burst_incr;
477	
478	    axi_write_m2s.w.data(axi_data'range) <= axi_data;
479	    axi_write_m2s.w.strb <= to_strb(data_width=>axi_data_width);
480	
481	    axi_write_m2s.b.ready <= '1';
482	
483	
484	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
485	    -- Optimized implementation for single-beat packets.
486	    packet_length_gen : if packet_length_axi_beats = 1 generate
487	      signal merged_ready, merged_valid : std_ulogic := '0';
488	    begin
489	
490	      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
491	      handshake_merger_inst : entity common.handshake_merger
492	        generic map (
493	          num_interfaces => 2
494	        )
495	        port map (
496	          clk => clk,
497	          --
498	          input_ready(0) => segment_ready,
499	          input_ready(1) => axi_ready,
500	          input_valid(0) => segment_valid,
501	          input_valid(1) => axi_valid,
502	          --
503	          result_ready => merged_ready,
504	          result_valid => merged_valid
505	        );
506	
507	
508	      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
509	      handshake_splitter_inst : entity common.handshake_splitter
510	        generic map (
511	          num_interfaces => 2
512	        )
513	        port map (
514	          clk => clk,
515	          --
516	          input_ready => merged_ready,
517	          input_valid => merged_valid,
518	          --
519	          output_ready(0) => axi_write_s2m.aw.ready,
520	          output_ready(1) => axi_write_s2m.w.ready,
521	          output_valid(0) => axi_write_m2s.aw.valid,
522	          output_valid(1) => axi_write_m2s.w.valid
523	        );
524	
525	      axi_write_m2s.aw.addr(segment_address'range) <= segment_address;
526	
527	      -- Packet length one beat -> it is always the last beat.
528	      axi_write_m2s.w.last <= '1';
529	
530	
531	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
532	    -- General implementation for multi-beat packets.
533	    else generate
534	      type state_t is (wait_for_start_condition, let_data_pass);
535	      signal state : state_t := wait_for_start_condition;
536	
537	      signal axi_last : std_ulogic := '0';
538	    begin
539	
540	      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
541	      address_handshaking : process
542	      begin
543	        wait until rising_edge(clk);
544	
545	        segment_ready <= '0';
546	
547	        if axi_write_s2m.aw.ready then
548	          axi_write_m2s.aw.valid <= '0';
549	        end if;
550	
551	        case state is
552	          when wait_for_start_condition =>
553	            -- In order to initiate an AW transaction and let W data through, we need to make
554	            -- sure that
555	            -- 1. We have incoming stream data, since sending an AW transaction before data is
556	            --    quite ill-behaved in an AXI sense.
557	            --    Some AXI slaves might not like it.
558	            -- 2. We have a valid address to write to.
559	            --    Meaning, the user has initiated the ring buffer and it is not full.
560	            -- 3. The previous AW transaction is done, since we simply assert AWVALID and do
561	            --    not wait for a transaction before proceeding in the state machine.
562	            if axi_valid and segment_valid and not axi_write_m2s.aw.valid then
563	              axi_write_m2s.aw.valid <= '1';
564	              -- Sample the address since we will pop the 'segment' word straight away, whereas
565	              -- we don't know when the 'AW' transaction will happen.
566	              axi_write_m2s.aw.addr(segment_address'range) <= segment_address;
567	
568	              -- Since we spend at least one clock cycle in the other state, it is safe to pop like
569	              -- this and then look at 'segment_valid' as soon as we return to this state again.
570	              segment_ready <= '1';
571	
572	              state <= let_data_pass;
573	            end if;
574	
575	          when let_data_pass =>
576	            -- Use the 'ready' and 'valid' that are not gated by the 'state'.
577	            -- Saves a little bit of critical path.
578	            if axi_write_s2m.w.ready and axi_valid and axi_last then
579	              state <= wait_for_start_condition;
580	            end if;
581	
582	        end case;
583	      end process;
584	
585	
586	      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
587	      -- Incoming stream has no 'last' indicator (by design).
588	      -- So we need to generate it internally.
589	      assign_last_inst : entity common.assign_last
590	        generic map (
591	          packet_length_beats => axi_burst_length_beats
592	        )
593	        port map (
594	          clk => clk,
595	          --
596	          ready => axi_ready,
597	          valid => axi_valid,
598	          last => axi_last
599	        );
600	
601	      axi_write_m2s.w.valid <= axi_valid and to_sl(state = let_data_pass);
602	      axi_write_m2s.w.last <= axi_last;
603	
604	      axi_ready <= axi_write_s2m.w.ready and to_sl(state = let_data_pass);
605	
606	    end generate;
607	
608	  end block;
609	
610	end architecture;
611

file.content:
-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Copyright (c) Lukas Vik. All rights reserved.
--
-- This file is part of the hdl-modules project, a collection of reusable, high-quality,
-- peer-reviewed VHDL building blocks.
-- https://hdl-modules.com
-- https://github.com/hdl-modules/hdl-modules
-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Main implementation of the simple DMA functionality.
-- This entity is not suitable for instantiation in a user design, use instead e.g.
-- :ref:`dma_axi_write_simple.dma_axi_write_simple_axi_lite`.
--
--
-- Packet length
-- _____________
--
-- The ``packet_length_beats`` generic specifies the packet length in terms of number of
-- input ``stream`` beats.
-- When one packet of streaming data has been written to DDR,
-- the ``write_done`` interrupt will trigger and the ``buffer_written_address`` register
-- will be updated.
-- This indicates to the software that there is data in the buffer that can be read.
--
-- .. note::
--   The packet length is a compile-time parameter.
--   It can not be changed during runtime and there is no support for writing or clearing
--   partial packets.
--
--   This saves a lot of resources and is part of the simple nature of this DMA core.
--
-- If the packet length specified by the user equates more than one maximum-length AXI burst,
-- the core will perform burst splitting internally.
--
--
-- Data width conversion
-- _____________________
--
-- The core supports data width conversion between the input ``stream`` and the AXI bus.
-- If the ``stream_data_width`` and ``axi_data_width`` are not equal,
-- :ref:`common.width_conversion` will be instantiated for a lightweight conversion.
-- Note that ``axi_data_width`` must be the native width of the AXI port.
--
--
-- .. _dma_axi_write_simple_resource_usage:
--
-- Resource usage
-- ______________
--
-- The core has a simple design with the goal of low resource utilization in mind.
-- See :ref:`dma_axi_write_simple.dma_axi_write_simple_axi_lite.resource_utilization`
-- for some build numbers.
-- These numbers are incredibly low compared to some other implementations.
--
-- The special case when ``packet_length_beats`` is 1 has an optimized implementation that gives
-- even lower resource usage than the general case.
-- This comes at the cost of quite poor memory performance, since every data beat becomes and
-- AXI burst in that case.
--
--
-- .. _dma_axi_write_simple_throughput:
--
-- AXI/data throughput
-- ___________________
--
-- The core has a one-cycle overhead per packet.
-- Meaning that for each packet, the input ``stream`` will stall (``stream_ready = 0``) for one
-- clock cycle.
-- This is assuming that ``AWREADY`` and ``WREADY`` are high.
-- If they are not, their stall will be propagated to the ``stream``.
--
-- This performance should be enough for even the most demanding applications.
-- The one-cycle overhead could theoretically be optimized away, but it is quite likely
-- that downstream AXI interconnect infrastructure has some overhead for each address
-- transaction anyway.
-- I.e. the one-cycle overhead in this core is probably not limiting the throughput overall.
--
-- If the memory buffer is full, the ``stream`` will stall until there is space.
-- When the software writes an updated ``buffer_read_address`` register indicating available space,
-- the ``stream`` will start after two clock cycles.
--
--
-- AXI behavior
-- ____________
--
-- The core is designed to be as well-behaved as possible in an AXI sense:
--
-- 1. AXI bursts of the maximum length possible will be used.
--
-- 2. The ``AW`` transaction is only initiated once we have at least one ``W`` beat available.
--
-- 3. ``BREADY`` is always high.
--
-- This gives very good AXI performance.
--
-- W channel block
-- ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
--
-- Related to bullet point 2 above, the core does NOT accumulate a whole burst in order to
-- guarantee no holes in the data.
-- Meaning, it is possible that an ``AW`` and a few ``W`` transactions  happen, but then the
-- ``stream`` might stop for a while and block the AXI bus before the burst is finished.
--
-- This can be problematic if the downstream AXI slave is a crossbar/interconnect that
-- arbitrates between multiple AXI masters.
--
-- It is up to the user to make sure that either,
--
-- 1. The ``stream`` stopping within a packet is rare enough that sufficient AXI performance
--    is reached.
-- 2. Or, the downstream AXI slave can handle holes without impacting performance.
--    :ref:`axi.axi_write_throttle` is designed to help with this.
--
-- AXI3
-- ~~~~
--
-- Setting the ``enable_axi3`` generic will make the core compliant with AXI3 instead of AXI4.
-- The core does not use any of the ID fields (``AWID``, ``WID``, ``BID``) so the only difference
-- is the burst length limitation.
--
--
-- ``write_done`` interrupt aggregation
-- ____________________________________
--
-- The ``write_done`` interrupt bit is triggered every time a packet has been written to memory
-- (see :ref:`dma_axi_write_simple.register_interface`).
-- If an interrupt-based control flow is used, this can lead to a high interrupt rate if
-- packets are small but data rate is high.
-- This can be problematic for the CPU.
--
-- The generics ``write_done_aggregate_count`` and ``write_done_aggregate_ticks`` can be used to
-- aggregate the interrupt event so it is triggered more sparsely.
-- See :ref:`common.event_aggregator` for details.
-- Commonly, both generics are set to ensure that the interrupt is triggered:
--
-- 1. Not too often (too little data per interrupt, too high overhead in interrupt manager).
-- 2. Not too seldom (too much data per interrupt, buffer might fill up and stream might stall).
-- 3. Not too delayed (too high latency in data flow).
-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

library ieee;
use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
use ieee.numeric_std.all;

library axi;
use axi.axi_pkg.all;

library axi_lite;
use axi_lite.axi_lite_pkg.all;

library common;
use common.types_pkg.all;

library math;
use math.math_pkg.is_power_of_two;

library register_file;
use register_file.register_file_pkg.all;

library ring_buffer;
use ring_buffer.ring_buffer_write_simple_pkg.all;

use work.dma_axi_write_simple_register_record_pkg.all;
use work.dma_axi_write_simple_regs_pkg.all;


entity dma_axi_write_simple is
  generic (
    -- The width of the AXI AWADDR field as well as all the ring buffer addresses
    -- handled internally.
    address_width : axi_address_width_t;
    -- The data width of the 'stream' interface.
    stream_data_width : axi_data_width_t;
    -- The width of the AXI WDATA field.
    -- Must be the native width of the AXI port, we do not support narrow bursts.
    axi_data_width : axi_data_width_t;
    -- The number of beats on the 'stream' interface that are accumulated before
    -- being written to memory.
    -- Increasing this up to the maximum burst size will improve memory performance.
    -- It will also decrease the frequency of 'write_done' interrupts.
    -- But note that there is no support for writing partial packets.
    -- If the 'stream' stops in the middle of a packet, there is not way to write or clear the
    -- accumulated data.
    packet_length_beats : positive;
    -- Enable AXI3 instead of AXI4, with the limitations that this implies.
    enable_axi3 : boolean;
    -- If set, the 'write_done' interrupt bit will be delayed until this many packets have
    -- been written to memory.
    -- See header/documentation for details.
    write_done_aggregate_count : positive;
    -- If set, the 'write_done' interrupt bit will be delayed and sent out at a periodic interval
    -- given by this clock tick count.
    -- See header/documentation for details.
    write_done_aggregate_ticks : positive
  );
  port (
    clk : in std_ulogic;
    --# {{}}
    stream_ready : out std_ulogic := '0';
    stream_valid : in std_ulogic;
    stream_data : in std_ulogic_vector(stream_data_width - 1 downto 0);
    --# {{}}
    regs_up : out dma_axi_write_simple_regs_up_t := dma_axi_write_simple_regs_up_init;
    regs_down : in dma_axi_write_simple_regs_down_t;
    interrupt : out std_ulogic := '0';
    --# {{}}
    axi_write_m2s : out axi_write_m2s_t := axi_write_m2s_init;
    axi_write_s2m : in axi_write_s2m_t
  );
end entity;

architecture a of dma_axi_write_simple is

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- Generic constants.
  constant stream_data_width_bytes : positive := stream_data_width / 8;
  constant packet_length_bytes : positive := packet_length_beats * stream_data_width_bytes;

  constant axi_data_width_bytes : positive := axi_data_width / 8;
  constant packet_length_axi_beats : positive := packet_length_bytes / axi_data_width_bytes;

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- Signals.
  signal interrupt_sources : register_t := (others => '0');

  signal ring_buffer_status : ring_buffer_write_simple_status_t := (
    ring_buffer_write_simple_status_idle_no_error
  );

  signal axi_ready, axi_valid : std_ulogic := '0';
  signal axi_data : std_ulogic_vector(axi_data_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');

begin

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  assert sanity_check_axi_data_width(data_width=>axi_data_width)
    report "Invalid AXI data width. See above."
    severity failure;

  -- We check the data width using the same function, for simplicity.
  assert sanity_check_axi_data_width(data_width=>stream_data_width)
    report "Invalid stream data width. See above."
    severity failure;

  assert stream_data_width mod axi_data_width = 0 or axi_data_width mod stream_data_width = 0
    report "Data width ratio must be a whole number of beats."
    severity failure;

  assert is_power_of_two(packet_length_beats)
    report "Packet length must be a power of two for efficient calculations."
    severity failure;

  assert packet_length_bytes mod axi_data_width_bytes = 0
    report "Packet length must be a whole number of AXI beats."
    severity failure;

  assert is_power_of_two(packet_length_bytes / axi_data_width_bytes)
    report "Packet length must be a power-of-two number of AXI beats."
    severity failure;


  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  interrupt_register_block : block
    signal write_done : std_ulogic := '0';
    signal clear, status : register_t := (others => '0');
  begin

    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    interrupt_register_inst : entity register_file.interrupt_register
      port map (
        clk => clk,
        --
        sources => interrupt_sources,
        mask => regs_down.interrupt_mask,
        clear => clear,
        --
        status => status,
        trigger => interrupt
      );

    write_done <= axi_write_m2s.b.ready and axi_write_s2m.b.valid;

    interrupt_sources(dma_axi_write_simple_interrupt_status_write_error) <= (
      axi_write_m2s.b.ready
      and axi_write_s2m.b.valid
      and to_sl(axi_write_s2m.b.resp /= axi_resp_okay)
    );

    interrupt_sources(dma_axi_write_simple_interrupt_status_start_address_unaligned_error) <= (
      ring_buffer_status.start_address_unaligned
    );

    interrupt_sources(dma_axi_write_simple_interrupt_status_end_address_unaligned_error) <= (
      ring_buffer_status.end_address_unaligned
    );

    interrupt_sources(dma_axi_write_simple_interrupt_status_read_address_unaligned_error) <= (
      ring_buffer_status.read_address_unaligned
    );

    clear <= to_slv(regs_down.interrupt_status);

    regs_up.interrupt_status <= to_dma_axi_write_simple_interrupt_status(status);


    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Optionally aggregate, is a passthrough if generics are at default value.
    event_aggregator_inst : entity common.event_aggregator
      generic map (
        event_count => write_done_aggregate_count,
        tick_count => write_done_aggregate_ticks
      )
      port map (
        clk => clk,
        --
        input_event => write_done,
        aggregated_event => interrupt_sources(dma_axi_write_simple_interrupt_status_write_done)
      );

  end block;


  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  width_conversion_gen : if stream_data_width /= axi_data_width generate

    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    width_conversion_inst : entity common.width_conversion
      generic map (
        input_width => stream_data'length,
        output_width => axi_data'length
      )
      port map (
        clk => clk,
        --
        input_ready => stream_ready,
        input_valid => stream_valid,
        input_data => stream_data,
        --
        output_ready => axi_ready,
        output_valid => axi_valid,
        output_data => axi_data
      );

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  else generate

    stream_ready <= axi_ready;
    axi_valid <= stream_valid;
    axi_data <= stream_data;

  end generate;


  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  axi_block : block
    function get_num_axi_bursts_per_packet return positive is
      constant max_axi_burst_length_beats : positive := get_max_burst_length_beats(
        enable_axi3=>enable_axi3
      );
      constant max_axi_burst_length_bytes : positive := (
        max_axi_burst_length_beats * axi_data_width_bytes
      );

      constant packet_fits_in_one_axi_burst : boolean := (
        packet_length_axi_beats <= max_axi_burst_length_beats
      );
    begin
      if packet_fits_in_one_axi_burst then
        return 1;
      end if;

      assert packet_length_bytes mod max_axi_burst_length_bytes = 0
        report (
            "When burst splitting, packet length must be a "
            & "whole number of max-length AXI bursts."
          )
          severity failure;

      return packet_length_bytes / max_axi_burst_length_bytes;
    end function;
    constant num_axi_bursts_per_packet : positive := get_num_axi_bursts_per_packet;

    constant axi_burst_length_beats : positive := (
      packet_length_axi_beats / num_axi_bursts_per_packet
    );
    constant axi_burst_length_bytes : positive := axi_burst_length_beats * axi_data_width_bytes;

    signal segment_ready, segment_valid : std_ulogic := '0';
    signal segment_address : u_unsigned(address_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
  begin

    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    print_things : process
    begin
      report "packet_length_beats = " & integer'image(packet_length_beats);
      report "packet_length_axi_beats = " & integer'image(packet_length_axi_beats);
      report "num_axi_bursts_per_packet = " & integer'image(num_axi_bursts_per_packet);
      report "axi_burst_length_beats = " & integer'image(axi_burst_length_beats);

      wait;
    end process;


    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    ring_buffer_block : block
      signal buffer_start_address, buffer_end_address, buffer_written_address, buffer_read_address :
        u_unsigned(address_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');

      -- If we are doing burst splitting, not every 'BVALID' marks the end of a packet.
      signal is_last_burst_in_packet : std_ulogic := '0';
      signal write_done : std_ulogic := '0';
    begin

      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
      ring_buffer_write_simple_inst : entity ring_buffer.ring_buffer_write_simple
        generic map (
          address_width => address_width,
          -- We pop one address 'segment' per AXI burst, which might be multiple per packet.
          segment_length_bytes => axi_burst_length_bytes,
          -- Update 'buffer_written_address' once the whole packet (not the 'segment')
          -- has been written.
          segments_per_packet => num_axi_bursts_per_packet
        )
        port map (
          clk => clk,
          --
          enable => regs_down.config.enable,
          --
          buffer_start_address => buffer_start_address,
          buffer_end_address => buffer_end_address,
          buffer_written_address => buffer_written_address,
          buffer_read_address => buffer_read_address,
          --
          segment_ready => segment_ready,
          segment_valid => segment_valid,
          segment_address => segment_address,
          --
          write_done => write_done,
          --
          status => ring_buffer_status
        );

      buffer_start_address <= u_unsigned(
        regs_down.buffer_start_address(buffer_start_address'range)
      );
      buffer_end_address <= u_unsigned(regs_down.buffer_end_address(buffer_end_address'range));
      buffer_read_address <= u_unsigned(regs_down.buffer_read_address(buffer_read_address'range));

      regs_up.buffer_written_address(buffer_written_address'range) <= std_ulogic_vector(
        buffer_written_address
      );


      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
      assign_last_inst : entity common.assign_last
        generic map (
          packet_length_beats => num_axi_bursts_per_packet
        )
        port map (
          clk => clk,
          --
          ready => axi_write_m2s.b.ready,
          valid => axi_write_s2m.b.valid,
          last => is_last_burst_in_packet
        );

      write_done <= (
        axi_write_m2s.b.ready and axi_write_s2m.b.valid and is_last_burst_in_packet
      );

    end block;

    -- Note that no AWID is set. Hence no WID has to be set in AXI3 mode either.

    axi_write_m2s.aw.len <= to_len(burst_length_beats=>axi_burst_length_beats);
    axi_write_m2s.aw.size <= to_size(data_width_bits=>axi_data_width);
    axi_write_m2s.aw.burst <= axi_a_burst_incr;

    axi_write_m2s.w.data(axi_data'range) <= axi_data;
    axi_write_m2s.w.strb <= to_strb(data_width=>axi_data_width);

    axi_write_m2s.b.ready <= '1';


    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Optimized implementation for single-beat packets.
    packet_length_gen : if packet_length_axi_beats = 1 generate
      signal merged_ready, merged_valid : std_ulogic := '0';
    begin

      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
      handshake_merger_inst : entity common.handshake_merger
        generic map (
          num_interfaces => 2
        )
        port map (
          clk => clk,
          --
          input_ready(0) => segment_ready,
          input_ready(1) => axi_ready,
          input_valid(0) => segment_valid,
          input_valid(1) => axi_valid,
          --
          result_ready => merged_ready,
          result_valid => merged_valid
        );


      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
      handshake_splitter_inst : entity common.handshake_splitter
        generic map (
          num_interfaces => 2
        )
        port map (
          clk => clk,
          --
          input_ready => merged_ready,
          input_valid => merged_valid,
          --
          output_ready(0) => axi_write_s2m.aw.ready,
          output_ready(1) => axi_write_s2m.w.ready,
          output_valid(0) => axi_write_m2s.aw.valid,
          output_valid(1) => axi_write_m2s.w.valid
        );

      axi_write_m2s.aw.addr(segment_address'range) <= segment_address;

      -- Packet length one beat -> it is always the last beat.
      axi_write_m2s.w.last <= '1';


    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- General implementation for multi-beat packets.
    else generate
      type state_t is (wait_for_start_condition, let_data_pass);
      signal state : state_t := wait_for_start_condition;

      signal axi_last : std_ulogic := '0';
    begin

      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
      address_handshaking : process
      begin
        wait until rising_edge(clk);

        segment_ready <= '0';

        if axi_write_s2m.aw.ready then
          axi_write_m2s.aw.valid <= '0';
        end if;

        case state is
          when wait_for_start_condition =>
            -- In order to initiate an AW transaction and let W data through, we need to make
            -- sure that
            -- 1. We have incoming stream data, since sending an AW transaction before data is
            --    quite ill-behaved in an AXI sense.
            --    Some AXI slaves might not like it.
            -- 2. We have a valid address to write to.
            --    Meaning, the user has initiated the ring buffer and it is not full.
            -- 3. The previous AW transaction is done, since we simply assert AWVALID and do
            --    not wait for a transaction before proceeding in the state machine.
            if axi_valid and segment_valid and not axi_write_m2s.aw.valid then
              axi_write_m2s.aw.valid <= '1';
              -- Sample the address since we will pop the 'segment' word straight away, whereas
              -- we don't know when the 'AW' transaction will happen.
              axi_write_m2s.aw.addr(segment_address'range) <= segment_address;

              -- Since we spend at least one clock cycle in the other state, it is safe to pop like
              -- this and then look at 'segment_valid' as soon as we return to this state again.
              segment_ready <= '1';

              state <= let_data_pass;
            end if;

          when let_data_pass =>
            -- Use the 'ready' and 'valid' that are not gated by the 'state'.
            -- Saves a little bit of critical path.
            if axi_write_s2m.w.ready and axi_valid and axi_last then
              state <= wait_for_start_condition;
            end if;

        end case;
      end process;


      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
      -- Incoming stream has no 'last' indicator (by design).
      -- So we need to generate it internally.
      assign_last_inst : entity common.assign_last
        generic map (
          packet_length_beats => axi_burst_length_beats
        )
        port map (
          clk => clk,
          --
          ready => axi_ready,
          valid => axi_valid,
          last => axi_last
        );

      axi_write_m2s.w.valid <= axi_valid and to_sl(state = let_data_pass);
      axi_write_m2s.w.last <= axi_last;

      axi_ready <= axi_write_s2m.w.ready and to_sl(state = let_data_pass);

    end generate;

  end block;

end architecture;

1	################################################################################
2	[interrupt_status]
3	
4	mode = "r_wpulse"
5	description = """
6	Interrupt status for the different interrupt sources of this module.
7	When an interrupt condition occurs, the corresponding bit in this register will read as '1' until
8	cleared (see below).
9	If the corresponding bit in **interrupt_mask** is also set to '1' by software, the **interrupt**
10	signal of this module will trigger, meaning it will be '1' until the interrupt(s) that triggered it
11	are cleared.
12	
13	Note that if an interrupt-based workflow is not used, this register can instead be polled
14	to check for events.
15	
16	Interrupt status is cleared by writing '1' to the target interrupt bit(s) in this register.
17	"""
18	
19	write_done.type = "bit"
20	write_done.description = """
21	A streaming packet, as defined by the **packet_length_beats** generic, has been written to memory.
22	Compare **buffer_written_address** with **buffer_read_address** to find out
23	how many bytes have been written and to which location.
24	"""
25	
26	write_error.type = "bit"
27	write_error.description = "Memory write responded with error (BRESP)."
28	
29	start_address_unaligned_error.type = "bit"
30	start_address_unaligned_error.description = """
31	The provided **buffer_start_address** is not aligned with the packet length.
32	"""
33	
34	end_address_unaligned_error.type = "bit"
35	end_address_unaligned_error.description = """
36	The provided **buffer_end_address** is not aligned with the packet length.
37	"""
38	
39	read_address_unaligned_error.type = "bit"
40	read_address_unaligned_error.description = """
41	The provided **buffer_read_address** is not aligned with the packet length.
42	"""
43	
44	
45	################################################################################
46	[interrupt_mask]
47	
48	mode = "r_w"
49	description = """
50	Interrupt enable mask for the different interrupts of this module.
51	The bits of this register correspond to the interrupts in **interrupt_status**.
52	
53	Clearing the mask of an interrupt will lower the **interrupt** signal of this module, if the
54	interrupt trigger was caused by that specific interrupt.
55	"""
56	
57	
58	################################################################################
59	[config]
60	
61	mode = "r_w"
62	description = "Configuration register."
63	
64	enable.type = "bit"
65	enable.description = """
66	When this bit is set, the module will continuously consume streaming data and write it to the
67	memory buffer.
68	Before this bit is set, the module will stall streaming data (**stream_ready** tied low).
69	
70	Before this bit is set, the **buffer_start_address**, **buffer_end_address**,
71	and **buffer_read_address** registers must be set with valid values.
72	
73	The module does not support disabling after enabling.
74	I.e, once this bit has been set, clearing it will result in an undefined behavior.
75	"""
76	
77	
78	################################################################################
79	[buffer_start_address]
80	
81	mode = "w"
82	description = """
83	Address to the first byte in the memory buffer.
84	Must be aligned by the packet length expressed in bytes.
85	
86	This address and upcoming bytes after will be written with data.
87	
88	Note that while a 32-bit value can be written to this register, only the number of
89	bits given by the **address_width** generic will actually be used by the module.
90	
91	Once this value has been set, and the module **enable**'d, the value must not be changed.
92	"""
93	
94	
95	################################################################################
96	[buffer_end_address]
97	
98	mode = "w"
99	description = """
100	Address to the byte after the last byte in the memory buffer.
101	Must be aligned by the packet length expressed in bytes.
102	
103	Bytes before this address (excluding) will be written with data.
104	
105	Note that while a 32-bit value can be written to this register, only the number of
106	bits given by the **address_width** generic will actually be used by the module.
107	
108	Once this value has been set, and the module **enable**'d, the value must not be changed.
109	"""
110	
111	
112	################################################################################
113	[buffer_written_address]
114	
115	mode = "r"
116	description = """
117	Is continuously updated by the module as data is written to the memory buffer.
118	All bytes between **buffer_read_address** (including) and this address (excluding) will contain
119	valid written data.
120	That number of bytes will always be a multiple of the burst length expressed in bytes.
121	
122	If **buffer_read_address** and this address are equal, the memory buffer is empty.
123	If they have different values, the memory buffer contains valid written data.
124	
125	When the module reaches the end of the buffer, i.e. when the very last byte of the buffer has been
126	written, the value of this register will wrap around to **buffer_start_address**.
127	I.e. this register will never assume the value of **buffer_end_address**.
128	
129	The value of this register will always be aligned by the packet length expressed in bytes.
130	
131	Note that while a 32-bit value is read from this register, only the number of
132	bits given by the **address_width** generic will actually be set by the module.
133	The others will always read as zero.
134	"""
135	
136	
137	################################################################################
138	[buffer_read_address]
139	
140	mode = "w"
141	description = """
142	Must be continuously updated by the software as it consumes data from the memory buffer.
143	All bytes before (excluding) this address are considered free and can be written by the module.
144	
145	When data is written to the memory buffer, the software must consume all the data between this
146	address (including) and **buffer_written_address** (excluding).
147	Once this is done, the software must update this register with the value
148	of **buffer_written_address**.
149	When that is done, this part of the memory buffer can be written again by the module.
150	Meaning, at that point, software must either be fully finished with the data or have a copy of it.
151	
152	The value written to this register must be aligned by the packet length expressed in bytes.
153	
154	Note that while a 32-bit value can be written to this register, only the number of
155	bits given by the **address_width** generic will actually be used by the module.
156	"""

################################################################################
[interrupt_status]

mode = "r_wpulse"
description = """
Interrupt status for the different interrupt sources of this module.
When an interrupt condition occurs, the corresponding bit in this register will read as '1' until
cleared (see below).
If the corresponding bit in **interrupt_mask** is also set to '1' by software, the **interrupt**
signal of this module will trigger, meaning it will be '1' until the interrupt(s) that triggered it
are cleared.

Note that if an interrupt-based workflow is not used, this register can instead be polled
to check for events.

Interrupt status is cleared by writing '1' to the target interrupt bit(s) in this register.
"""

write_done.type = "bit"
write_done.description = """
A streaming packet, as defined by the **packet_length_beats** generic, has been written to memory.
Compare **buffer_written_address** with **buffer_read_address** to find out
how many bytes have been written and to which location.
"""

write_error.type = "bit"
write_error.description = "Memory write responded with error (BRESP)."

start_address_unaligned_error.type = "bit"
start_address_unaligned_error.description = """
The provided **buffer_start_address** is not aligned with the packet length.
"""

end_address_unaligned_error.type = "bit"
end_address_unaligned_error.description = """
The provided **buffer_end_address** is not aligned with the packet length.
"""

read_address_unaligned_error.type = "bit"
read_address_unaligned_error.description = """
The provided **buffer_read_address** is not aligned with the packet length.
"""


################################################################################
[interrupt_mask]

mode = "r_w"
description = """
Interrupt enable mask for the different interrupts of this module.
The bits of this register correspond to the interrupts in **interrupt_status**.

Clearing the mask of an interrupt will lower the **interrupt** signal of this module, if the
interrupt trigger was caused by that specific interrupt.
"""


################################################################################
[config]

mode = "r_w"
description = "Configuration register."

enable.type = "bit"
enable.description = """
When this bit is set, the module will continuously consume streaming data and write it to the
memory buffer.
Before this bit is set, the module will stall streaming data (**stream_ready** tied low).

Before this bit is set, the **buffer_start_address**, **buffer_end_address**,
and **buffer_read_address** registers must be set with valid values.

The module does not support disabling after enabling.
I.e, once this bit has been set, clearing it will result in an undefined behavior.
"""


################################################################################
[buffer_start_address]

mode = "w"
description = """
Address to the first byte in the memory buffer.
Must be aligned by the packet length expressed in bytes.

This address and upcoming bytes after will be written with data.

Note that while a 32-bit value can be written to this register, only the number of
bits given by the **address_width** generic will actually be used by the module.

Once this value has been set, and the module **enable**'d, the value must not be changed.
"""


################################################################################
[buffer_end_address]

mode = "w"
description = """
Address to the byte after the last byte in the memory buffer.
Must be aligned by the packet length expressed in bytes.

Bytes before this address (excluding) will be written with data.

Note that while a 32-bit value can be written to this register, only the number of
bits given by the **address_width** generic will actually be used by the module.

Once this value has been set, and the module **enable**'d, the value must not be changed.
"""


################################################################################
[buffer_written_address]

mode = "r"
description = """
Is continuously updated by the module as data is written to the memory buffer.
All bytes between **buffer_read_address** (including) and this address (excluding) will contain
valid written data.
That number of bytes will always be a multiple of the burst length expressed in bytes.

If **buffer_read_address** and this address are equal, the memory buffer is empty.
If they have different values, the memory buffer contains valid written data.

When the module reaches the end of the buffer, i.e. when the very last byte of the buffer has been
written, the value of this register will wrap around to **buffer_start_address**.
I.e. this register will never assume the value of **buffer_end_address**.

The value of this register will always be aligned by the packet length expressed in bytes.

Note that while a 32-bit value is read from this register, only the number of
bits given by the **address_width** generic will actually be set by the module.
The others will always read as zero.
"""


################################################################################
[buffer_read_address]

mode = "w"
description = """
Must be continuously updated by the software as it consumes data from the memory buffer.
All bytes before (excluding) this address are considered free and can be written by the module.

When data is written to the memory buffer, the software must consume all the data between this
address (including) and **buffer_written_address** (excluding).
Once this is done, the software must update this register with the value
of **buffer_written_address**.
When that is done, this part of the memory buffer can be written again by the module.
Meaning, at that point, software must either be fully finished with the data or have a copy of it.

The value written to this register must be aligned by the packet length expressed in bytes.

Note that while a 32-bit value can be written to this register, only the number of
bits given by the **address_width** generic will actually be used by the module.
"""

file.content:
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Copyright (c) Lukas Vik. All rights reserved.
#
# This file is part of the hdl-modules project, a collection of reusable, high-quality,
# peer-reviewed VHDL building blocks.
# https://hdl-modules.com
# https://github.com/hdl-modules/hdl-modules
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any

from tsfpga.examples.vivado.project import TsfpgaExampleVivadoNetlistProject
from tsfpga.module import BaseModule
from tsfpga.vivado.build_result_checker import EqualTo, Ffs, MaximumLogicLevel, TotalLuts

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from vunit.ui import VUnit


class Module(BaseModule):
    def setup_vunit(
        self,
        vunit_proj: VUnit,
        **kwargs: Any,  # noqa: ANN401, ARG002
    ) -> None:
        test = vunit_proj.library(self.library_name).test_bench("tb_dma_axi_write_simple")
        self.add_vunit_config(test=test, count=8)

    def get_build_projects(self) -> list[TsfpgaExampleVivadoNetlistProject]:
        # The 'hdl_modules' Python package is probably not on the PYTHONPATH in most scenarios where
        # this module is used. Hence we can not import at the top of this file.
        # This method is only called when running netlist builds in the hdl-modules repo from the
        # bundled tools/build_fpga.py, where PYTHONPATH is correctly set up.
        from hdl_modules import get_hdl_modules  # noqa: PLC0415

        modules = get_hdl_modules()
        part = "xc7z020clg400-1"

        projects = []

        def add(generics: dict, lut: int, ff: int, logic: int) -> None:
            all_generics = dict(address_width=29, stream_data_width=64, **generics)
            projects.append(
                TsfpgaExampleVivadoNetlistProject(
                    name=self.netlist_build_name(
                        "dma_axi_write_simple_axi_lite", generics=all_generics
                    ),
                    modules=modules,
                    part=part,
                    top="dma_axi_write_simple_axi_lite",
                    generics=all_generics,
                    build_result_checkers=[
                        TotalLuts(EqualTo(lut)),
                        Ffs(EqualTo(ff)),
                        MaximumLogicLevel(EqualTo(logic)),
                    ],
                )
            )

        add(generics={"axi_data_width": 64, "packet_length_beats": 1}, lut=156, ff=207, logic=16)
        add(generics={"axi_data_width": 64, "packet_length_beats": 16}, lut=157, ff=226, logic=12)
        add(generics={"axi_data_width": 64, "packet_length_beats": 2048}, lut=132, ff=218, logic=11)
        add(
            generics={"axi_data_width": 64, "packet_length_beats": 16384}, lut=134, ff=218, logic=10
        )

        add(
            generics={"axi_data_width": 32, "packet_length_beats": 16384}, lut=171, ff=320, logic=11
        )
        add(
            generics={"axi_data_width": 128, "packet_length_beats": 16384},
            lut=198,
            ff=410,
            logic=11,
        )
        add(
            generics={
                "axi_data_width": 64,
                "packet_length_beats": 1024,
                "write_done_aggregate_count": 512,
                "write_done_aggregate_ticks": 262144,
            },
            lut=156,
            ff=247,
            logic=12,
        )

        return projects

content.content:
1	This module contains an open-source Direct Memory Access (DMA) component for
2	streaming data from FPGA to DDR memory over AXI.
3	Sometimes called "AXI DMA S2MM".
4	The implementation is optimized for
5	
6	1. very low :ref:`resource usage <dma_axi_write_simple_resource_usage>`, and
7	2. maximum :ref:`AXI/data throughput <dma_axi_write_simple_throughput>`.
8	
9	Being simplified, however, it has the following limitations:
10	
11	1. Can only write to continuous ring buffer space in DDR.
12	   No scatter-gather support.
13	2. Does not support data strobing or narrow AXI transfers.
14	   All addresses must be aligned with the AXI data width.
15	3. Uses a static compile-time packet length, with no support for partial packets.
16	4. Packet length must be power of two.
17	
18	These limitations and the simplicity of the design are intentional.
19	This is what enables the low resource usage and high throughput.
20	
21	
22	.. _dma_axi_write_simple_cpp:
23	
24	C++ driver
25	----------
26	
27	There is a complete C++ driver available in the
28	`cpp sub-folder <https://github.com/hdl-modules/hdl-modules/tree/main/modules/dma_axi_write_simple/cpp>`__
29	in the repository.
30	The class provides a convenient API for setting up the module and receiving stream data from
31	the FPGA.
32	It supports an interrupt-based as well as a polling-based workflow.
33	See the header file for documentation.
34	
35	
36	Simulate and build FPGA with register artifacts
37	-----------------------------------------------
38	
39	This module is controlled over a register bus, with code generated by
40	`hdl-registers <https://hdl-registers.com>`_.
41	See :ref:`dma_axi_write_simple.register_interface` for register documentation.
42	
43	Generated register code artifacts are not checked in to the repository.
44	The recommended way to use hdl-modules is with `tsfpga <https://tsfpga.com>`__
45	(see :ref:`getting_started`), in which case register code is always generated and kept up to date
46	automatically.
47	This is by far the most convenient and portable solution.
48	
49	If you dont't want to use tsfpga, you can integrate hdl-registers code generation in your
50	build/simulation flow or use the hard coded artifacts below (not recommended).
51	
52	
53	Hard coded artifacts
54	____________________
55	
56	Not recommended, but if you don't want to use tsfpga or hdl-registers,
57	these generated VHDL artifacts can be included in the ``dma_axi_write_simple`` library
58	for simulation and synthesis:
59	
60	1. :download:`regs_src/dma_axi_write_simple_regs_pkg.vhd <vhdl/dma_axi_write_simple_regs_pkg.vhd>`
61	2. :download:`regs_src/dma_axi_write_simple_register_record_pkg.vhd <vhdl/dma_axi_write_simple_register_record_pkg.vhd>`
62	3. :download:`regs_src/dma_axi_write_simple_register_file_axi_lite.vhd <vhdl/dma_axi_write_simple_register_file_axi_lite.vhd>`
63	4. :download:`regs_sim/dma_axi_write_simple_register_read_write_pkg.vhd <vhdl/dma_axi_write_simple_register_read_write_pkg.vhd>`
64	
65	The first few are source files that shall be included in your simulation as well build project.
66	The last one is a simulation file that shall be included only in your simulation project.
67	These generated C++ artifacts can be used to control the module from software:
68	
69	1. :download:`include/i_dma_axi_write_simple.h <cpp/include/i_dma_axi_write_simple.h>`
70	2. :download:`include/dma_axi_write_simple.h <cpp/include/dma_axi_write_simple.h>`
71	3. :download:`dma_axi_write_simple.cpp <cpp/dma_axi_write_simple.cpp>`
72	
73	.. warning::
74	   When copy-pasting generated artifacts, there is a large risk that things go out of sync when
75	   e.g. versions are bumped.
76	   An automated solution with :ref:`tsfpga <getting_started>` is highly recommended.
77

file.content:
This module contains an open-source Direct Memory Access (DMA) component for
streaming data from FPGA to DDR memory over AXI.
Sometimes called "AXI DMA S2MM".
The implementation is optimized for

1. very low :ref:`resource usage <dma_axi_write_simple_resource_usage>`, and
2. maximum :ref:`AXI/data throughput <dma_axi_write_simple_throughput>`.

Being simplified, however, it has the following limitations:

1. Can only write to continuous ring buffer space in DDR.
   No scatter-gather support.
2. Does not support data strobing or narrow AXI transfers.
   All addresses must be aligned with the AXI data width.
3. Uses a static compile-time packet length, with no support for partial packets.
4. Packet length must be power of two.

These limitations and the simplicity of the design are intentional.
This is what enables the low resource usage and high throughput.


.. _dma_axi_write_simple_cpp:

C++ driver
----------

There is a complete C++ driver available in the
`cpp sub-folder <https://github.com/hdl-modules/hdl-modules/tree/main/modules/dma_axi_write_simple/cpp>`__
in the repository.
The class provides a convenient API for setting up the module and receiving stream data from
the FPGA.
It supports an interrupt-based as well as a polling-based workflow.
See the header file for documentation.


Simulate and build FPGA with register artifacts
-----------------------------------------------

This module is controlled over a register bus, with code generated by
`hdl-registers <https://hdl-registers.com>`_.
See :ref:`dma_axi_write_simple.register_interface` for register documentation.

Generated register code artifacts are not checked in to the repository.
The recommended way to use hdl-modules is with `tsfpga <https://tsfpga.com>`__
(see :ref:`getting_started`), in which case register code is always generated and kept up to date
automatically.
This is by far the most convenient and portable solution.

If you dont't want to use tsfpga, you can integrate hdl-registers code generation in your
build/simulation flow or use the hard coded artifacts below (not recommended).


Hard coded artifacts
____________________

Not recommended, but if you don't want to use tsfpga or hdl-registers,
these generated VHDL artifacts can be included in the ``dma_axi_write_simple`` library
for simulation and synthesis:

1. :download:`regs_src/dma_axi_write_simple_regs_pkg.vhd <vhdl/dma_axi_write_simple_regs_pkg.vhd>`
2. :download:`regs_src/dma_axi_write_simple_register_record_pkg.vhd <vhdl/dma_axi_write_simple_register_record_pkg.vhd>`
3. :download:`regs_src/dma_axi_write_simple_register_file_axi_lite.vhd <vhdl/dma_axi_write_simple_register_file_axi_lite.vhd>`
4. :download:`regs_sim/dma_axi_write_simple_register_read_write_pkg.vhd <vhdl/dma_axi_write_simple_register_read_write_pkg.vhd>`

The first few are source files that shall be included in your simulation as well build project.
The last one is a simulation file that shall be included only in your simulation project.
These generated C++ artifacts can be used to control the module from software:

1. :download:`include/i_dma_axi_write_simple.h <cpp/include/i_dma_axi_write_simple.h>`
2. :download:`include/dma_axi_write_simple.h <cpp/include/dma_axi_write_simple.h>`
3. :download:`dma_axi_write_simple.cpp <cpp/dma_axi_write_simple.cpp>`

.. warning::
   When copy-pasting generated artifacts, there is a large risk that things go out of sync when
   e.g. versions are bumped.
   An automated solution with :ref:`tsfpga <getting_started>` is highly recommended.
